mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins, triggering negative equity returns as investors price in deteriorating earnings fundamentals.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.96.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction, prompting rapid risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum yields positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or payroll growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.3 percent month-over-month, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures widens negatively, indicating household spending fails to sustain broader expansion, which drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS spread fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent improvements in industrial production and declining unemployment during post-shock recovery phases indicate rapid capacity utilization, driving aggressive risk-on flows and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 40 percent of observations when both INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls during recovery regimes.
